assumption_text: Civilian exposure is modeled campaign-wide at the FRY level rather than city-by-city.
rationale: The strongest civilian casualty series is campaign-wide and the target set covered Serbia, Montenegro, and Kosovo.

assumption_text: Military exposure is bounded as active Yugoslav Army strength plus a limited special-police component, excluding the full reserve force and irregulars.
rationale: This creates a grounded floor instead of a false precise census.

assumption_text: Civilian stock is adjusted by 0, one-half, or all of the 862,979 documented expulsions of Kosovo Albanians.
rationale: Mass wartime displacement is documented, but no daily FRY-wide stock series was found.

assumption_text: Police deaths are included in the military-side numerator, while the denominator uses only a bounded police component rather than a full nationwide police census.
rationale: The best recoverable military death figure explicitly includes policemen, but same-window exposure counts for all police forces were not recovered.

assumption_text: The episode is treated as 78 days, ending 9 June 1999, to align with the HRW civilian incident frame, even though some commemorative sources place the last strike on 10 June.
rationale: This keeps the denominator window aligned to the strongest civilian numerator source.
